adversaries: domestic actors that resist militia escalation
strategic_objectives: stretch Israeli air-defense and reserve posture
retain domestic political relevance despite conflict costs
keep logistics links resilient under interdiction pressure

strengths: ability to blend military and political activity
preferred_instruments: localized escalation calibrated to Iranian signaling
political messaging through endurance and survivability
readiness_drivers: ability to preserve communications under heavy pressure

decision_doctrine.risk_tolerance: high for attritional border conflict, lower for full collapse of Lebanese support base
decision_doctrine.escalation_bias: prefer steady pressure and selective spikes over immediate all-out war
decision_doctrine.negotiation_posture: accepts pauses that preserve claim of resistance and leave survivable capacity intact
red_lines: loss of core political-control zones around Beirut and the Bekaa
visible break with Iran-backed deterrence logic
domestic legitimacy shock caused by unmanageable Lebanese destruction
off_ramps: pause that can be framed as deterrence success
reduced Israeli pressure on the central Lebanese political core
continued ability to claim survivability and relevance

economic_pressure_points: loss of launch areas in southern Lebanon
funding pressure from sanctions or interdiction
civilian backlash tied to prolonged destruction
degradation of commercial and social support networks in Beirut and the Bekaa
intelligence_priorities: which Lebanese zones remain politically and logistically permissive
how quickly Israel regenerates strike tempo on the northern front

priority_fronts.name: Southern Launch Belt
priority_fronts.goal: preserve enough launch access to sustain deterrence through persistence
trigger_indicators: civilian evacuation shifts near the border

priority_fronts.name: Bekaa Logistics Depth
priority_fronts.goal: keep material, personnel, and communications moving under pressure

priority_fronts.name: Beirut Political Core
priority_fronts.goal: retain command continuity and domestic relevance

priority_fronts.name: Syrian Transit Corridor
priority_fronts.goal: maintain external replenishment and strategic depth

strategic_relationships.dependency: weapons flow, strategic guidance, and deterrence framing
strategic_relationships.notes: Core source of legitimacy for regional resistance posture.

strategic_relationships.dependency: room for maneuver and political cover
strategic_relationships.notes: Necessary but unstable relationship shaped by conflict damage.

strategic_relationships.dependency: mutual deterrence through pain thresholds and signaling
strategic_relationships.notes: Hezbollah reads Israel through reserve tempo, evacuation policy, and interception patterns.
If degraded, Hezbollah becomes more cautious and survival-focused.

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Bekaa-Syria transit arc
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: porous enough for sustainment and movement

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Beirut southern-suburb political core
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: retained as a command and legitimacy anchor

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Litani south-north depth belt
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: usable as maneuver, dispersal, and concealment space
